postText:
Last night, the Shelbyville Common Council in Indiana approved annexing 429 acres on which a 900-MW Prologis data center campus will go.

It also approved a 30-year, 100% personal property tax abatement and a 10-year, 50 percent real property tax abatement. https://t.co/dxC7Xu59Om
bundle.chartTitle: Shelbyville Common Council advances plans for $2B data center complex
keyFindings: A major $2 billion data center project is advancing in Shelbyville, indicating a significant upcoming surge in localized electricity demand.
The project represents a substantial capital investment in digital infrastructure, which typically necessitates high-reliability power grid connections and potentially renewable energy sourcing.
Local government approval is a key milestone for large-scale energy-intensive developments like data center campuses.
